methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rivian is a public-stage transportation company with a current market valuation of $20.7B and $13.5B in cumulative capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of five private-stage transportation companies: The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace. No public comparables exist within a sufficiently narrow filtering criterion.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this analysis. A systematic screen for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers within the Transportation sector yielded insufficient datapoints—fewer than the three minimum required for reliable median estimation. While four same-sector peers exist in Transportation broadly, none met the specified stage or tier thresholds. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce material cross-sector distortion and is therefore not employed.

Given the constraints of the available dataset, this valuation cannot be supported by quantitative triangulation at this time. The primary limitation is the absence of publicly traded Transportation comparables and the early-stage nature of identified private peers relative to Rivian's public status. A defensible range would require either expansion of the comp set to include later-stage private Transportation companies or broader sector inclusion with explicit adjustments for stage and market maturity. Key uncertainties center on whether private Transportation peers trade at meaningful valuation premiums or discounts to Rivian's public multiple, and whether such adjustments are empirically justified.
